Ad Text:
OUR CLIENT

Our client is a well-renowned public health organisation located in Melbourne’s Eastern Suburbs. The Procurement function operates within a collaborative, values-driven environment focused on delivering strong commercial outcomes while ensuring compliance with Victorian Government procurement policies and frameworks.

With an increasing portfolio of capital projects and organisational transformation initiatives, the business is seeking a Procurement Process & Governance Manager to lead procurement excellence, governance uplift and support the delivery of complex procurement activities across Capital Projects including Construction, Refurbishments, FFE and Medical Equipment.

ROLE

As the Procurement Process & Governance Manager, you will play a critical role in strengthening procurement frameworks, policies and processes across the organisation. You will work closely with the GM Procurement and senior stakeholders to ensure procurement activities are compliant, efficient, and aligned with Victorian Government requirements while enabling effective service delivery outcomes for patients.

This role will see you driving governance improvements and supporting high-priority procurement initiatives beyond the day-to-day pipeline. You will engage broadly across the organisation, providing expert advice, uplifting capability and ensuring best practice procurement standards are embedded.
Projects within scope will range from governance and policy uplift through to supporting complex sourcing activities, including high-value and high-risk procurement processes requiring strong oversight and stakeholder engagement.

Specifically, you will:

Lead the development, implementation and continuous improvement of procurement policies, frameworks and governance processes
• Ensure compliance with Victorian Government procurement regulations, audit requirements and internal controls
• Provide expert procurement advice to senior stakeholders on governance, risk and probity matters
• Drive procurement capability uplift across the organisation through training, tools and process improvements
• Build strong internal relationships to ensure alignment between procurement strategy and business objectives
SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

The successful candidate will bring strong experience in procurement governance, process improvement and complex sourcing within regulated environments. You will be comfortable working with senior stakeholders, operating autonomously while contributing to broader strategic objectives led by the GM Procurement.

You are a structured and commercially astute professional who can balance governance requirements with practical delivery outcomes. Experience within public sector or health is highly regarded, particularly with an understanding of Victorian Government procurement frameworks.

5+ years’ experience in Procurement, with a focus on governance, process or category management
• Strong knowledge of public sector procurement frameworks (Victorian Government experience highly desirable)
• Proven ability to design and implement procurement policies, processes and controls
• High attention to detail with strong risk management and compliance capability
• Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills
• Ability to operate autonomously while managing multiple priorities
GET IN TOUCH

If you require more information on the role or if you have any further questions, please contact Rory O’Sullivan via email at rory@loop-recruit.com

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - Cyber Security - 6 months + $900 - $1,000 per day
Ad Text:
Senior Business Analyst – 6 months - $850 - $1,000 per day – 2 days in office

Senior Business Analyst with cybersecurity project experience to support a cybersecurity uplift program.

The Client
Financial Services business investing in a major cyber/security uplift. Very flexible, hybrid work model and supportive culture.

The Role

Deliver business analysis for a range of projects (requirements, process mapping, stakeholder engagement, including IDAM, network hardening, Security Operations Uplift.

Your Background

Senior Business Analyst with cyber/security or enterprise tech project experience

Strong end-to-end business analysis skills

Comfortable working across multiple streams

Experienced in leading workshops with business stakeholders and vendors

Knowledge of cybersecurity frameworks and security standards

Relevant certifications such as AgileBA, CBAP, CISSP, or CISM (not essential)

Why Join?

Lead the business analysis for a high-impact cybersecurity and tech uplift program (ITSM, IDAM)

Flexible, hybrid work (2 days in office)

Likely extension

Apply Now

If you are ready to take on a challenging and rewarding role as a Senior Business Analyst, apply now to be considered for this opportunity.

Your work days are brighter here.

We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.

About the Team

As a member of our diverse and skilled ANZ Solution Consulting team, you will have the opportunity to communicate and demonstrate the power of Workday's applications to prospects and customers alike. It is this team's job to bring the value of Workday solutions to life and showcase how the world of work can be transformed. Along with working closely with our sales counterparts, this team also sits at the exciting intersection of our Product Management, Consulting Services, Marketing, and Value Management teams, providing exposure to various areas of the business.

About the Role

We have an exciting opportunity for a Financials Solution Consultant for our ANZ team! Our team demonstrates the value of next generation SaaS Financial Management Applications and Artificial Intelligence Agents to Workday prospects and customers. Through interactive pre-sales discovery meetings, and by leveraging deep Financial Accounting skills and complex Enterprise Software product knowledge, Workday Solution Consultants provide Financials expertise, Executive presentations, and Workday Financials product demonstrations, in partnership with our sales team, to customers throughout the sales cycle. Our team works in a highly complex environment that requires curiosity, flexibility, and drive.  Workday is looking for a Solution Consultant with Financials & ERP expertise to join ANZ Solution Consultant team working across medium and larger enterprise customers. 

About You

Basic Qualifications

Experience across finance and accounting domains, including  GL, AR, AP, Fixed Assets, Multi-Company and Consolidations, Multi-Currency
5+ years of functional, implementation, consulting, or presales experience with Financial Management or ERP solutions.
Strong understanding of additional Finance-related functions such as Procurement, Project Costing is beneficial
Proven experience engaging with enterprise finance stakeholders, including Controllers, CFOs, and Finance Transformation leaders.
Exposure to Financial Management solutions from Workday, Oracle, SAP, Microsoft or similar would be an advantage
Experience in engagements with enterprises in Australia and New Zealand across one or more of the following industries - FSI, Higher Education, Healthcare, Government and Public Services, Retail, Professional Services etc.

Other Qualifications / Profile

You are a credible, dynamic finance transformation expert, ready to help organizations sell and operate the future of finance today.
An outstanding and confident presenter with strong communication and influencing skills, capable of engaging and persuading CFOs, Finance VPs, Controllers, and senior IT leaders.
Extensive experience in the finance and ERP technology space, with deep knowledge of Financial Management, ERP, EPM, and analytics platforms, and how they are applied to modernize finance operations.
Strong passion for AI, machine learning, automation, and agentic AI, with a clear point of view on how these technologies are transforming finance processes, insights, and decision-making.
Genuinely excited about Workday Financial Management and motivated to represent Workday’s next-generation AI-powered finance capabilities to the market.
Deep understanding of the finance operating model, enabling you to speak fluently with the Office of the CFO about governance, controls, efficiency, agility, and business outcomes—not just system features.
Proven ability to map customer-specific finance challenges directly to technology and AI driven solutions, articulating value in terms of faster close, improved visibility, stronger compliance, and reduced manual effort—rather than simply listing product functionality.

Our Approach to Flexible Work
 

With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.

At Workday, we are committed to providing an accessible and inclusive hiring experience where all candidates can fully demonstrate their skills. If you require assistance or an accommodation at any point, please email accommodations@workday.com.

In addition, Workday will never ask candidates to pay a recruiting fee, or pay for consulting or coaching services, in order to apply for a job at Workday.
